counters.counter_desc: this is one of the heroes that ragebaits me as a Natan user, not only this Hero has a short cooldown but it's just so hard to kill it and his ultimate is so so annoying we could even pick off Natan in 1v1 situation even in late game.

it can easily pick you up early to mid game and that thing makes you slow on farming growth, but if you're slowly get golds from assists kills or even small objectives you could take him down eventually

dominants.dominant_desc: if you already have two or three items made make sure you chase down objectives such as turtles lords and even turrets, you could pick off enemy heroes but make sure you read the enemy rotations and location

let's say you already have full item full buff and protection, you have to take a risk, by using your ultimate to quickly change your position and use your first kill then second skill to burst and CC the enemy hero you could pick off them and if you combo it with basic attacks you could take down many enemy heroes but make sure your position is guarded and free. 

but if you failed to wipe out enemy Heroes it's still all right because you gave your team info that enemy heroes are now crippled and it's their job to do the the rest or you already take down their core.

emblems.emblem_desc:
people will instantly point pull yourself together, well my answer is Natan needs retribution to chase down enemy heroes and his jungling speed ability is so hard that I don't think he need seasoned junter to use for jungling. so that's why I need short cooldown on retribution (up to 28 sec cooldown).

but this emblem depends on situations sometimes you need seasoned hunter and sometimes you defaultly use this emblem, I would recommend using this emblem if your enemy heroes is not a pickoff type/early game aggressors

equips.equip_desc: this is the default equipment I use it has attack speed and high magic damage that you could rely on to chase down enemy and even meltdown objectives, but if you need lifesteals you could swap windtalker with concentrated energy

hero.hero_overview:
Natan in this meta is a very flexible pick, most people will do Gold but I'd say if you really want to counter Yi Sun Shin or have a late game holder jungler Natan is the best pick for me.

Why did I say flexible pick in the first place?
that's because Natan can be goldlane or jungling in various situations and could even compete with the current jungling meta.
hero.hero_strength:
Natan is a late game type core, whatever you do always farm until you get at least two or three main items so you could ace the game. Natan could surpass his enemy jungling because of his passive and short ultimate cooldown, and keep in mind high of Attack Speed. 

Nathan can also "thanos snap" down squishy enemy heroes using his 1st skill and ultimate, he can also melt down tanks heroes in a matter of seconds. 

And if you ask about objectives, well ladies and gentlemen Natan is the key of them all!
Turrets? gone, Enemy heroes? gone, and even Lord and turtle can be melted down in less than 10 seconds!.

So if your feeling ulset that your buffs and creeps are getting stolen, that's okay because Nathan can also farm in various way possible and in the future he can also ace the game.

this is just another Ixia Diggie meta strategy but the slight difference is you can get more than one late game core, but better because Natan doesn't have to rely on diggie when he reached late game, though there are some other alternatives for supports
hero.hero_weakness:
Natan is really squishy, he needs help in his early game farming ability and like I said before he couldn't do anything before his second or third item was made.

Natan is also really really fragile meaning he could Ace the game but if he got picked off in early or even late game then congrats youre cooked, so I recommended Carmilla or Minotaur as his companion from early game to late game.

and if your playing Natan you couldn't just "innocently" wanders of the lane because once again Natan is a late game core and if you don't have another late game core, the only hope is just Natan.

FYI: Natan's movement speed so slow I even cried how he walked nonchalantly when his teammates getting clapped by 5 peoples

data.recommend: How to ace the jungle using Natan, don't forget to like guys because it's free LMAO.

spell.spell_desc:
I chose retribution because well this tutorial is for jungling and what's up with people that are jungling without using retribution lmao.

make sure to use blue retribution because well you need movement speed to rotate around the lanes an escape from getting picked off or even chase enemy heroes.

data.title: Ace the Jungle - Natan

cooperates.cooperate_desc: Works well with tank or crowd control heroes like Tigreal or Atlas — they help Natan deal safe and consistent damage.

counters.counter_desc: Struggles against assassins like Hayabusa or Ling who can burst him before he can react.

emblems.emblem_desc: The Marksman Emblem is the best choice for Natan because it enhances his attack power and mobility, which are both crucial for a marksman hero. The extra movement speed helps him kite enemies more effectively, while the Weapon Master talent increases his total damage output. This emblem setup gives Natan a strong balance of power and survivability, helping him dominate in the late game.

equips.equip_desc: High attack speed and lifesteal for strong late-game damage. Perfect for continuous fights.

hero.hero_overview: Natan is a marksman hero who deals both physical and magic damage, making him one of the most unique heroes in Mobile Legends. He excels in long-range fights and can quickly turn the tide of battle using his ultimate skill, which creates a reverse clone that mirrors his attacks. Natan is a late-game powerhouse who can melt enemies with his high attack speed and precise positioning. His skills allow him to kite opponents while continuously dealing heavy burst damage.
hero.hero_strength:
Strong late-game scaling and incredible burst damage

Deals both physical and magic damage, making him hard to counter

Excellent mobility and kiting potential using Skill 2

Ultimate clone doubles his damage output in team fights

Great at cleaning up fights and chasing down enemies
hero.hero_weakness:
Weak early game and relies on items to deal consistent damage

Very squishy and easily killed if caught off guard

Requires good positioning and awareness in every fight

Ultimate needs precise timing to be effective

Struggles against assassins and burst heroes

spell.spell_desc: Flicker is the best battle spell for Natan because it helps him reposition during fights or escape from dangerous situations. Since Natan is very squishy and has no dash skill outside of his second skill, Flicker gives him extra mobility and survivability. It can also be used offensively to chase low-HP enemies after using his ultimate.

data.title: Natan the great

cooperates.cooperate_desc: Offers both mobility and protection.  Her Guiding Wind allows Natan to reposition quickly or escape danger, while her crowd control and damage boost enhance his combat effectiveness.
Provides crucial healing and sustain, allowing Natan to stay in fights longer and maximize his damage output.  His slow also helps Natan land his skill shots.
- Edith:  Provides strong frontline protection and crowd control, creating space for Natan to deal damage safely from the backline.
- Atlas:  His ultimate skill, Fatal Links, can group enemies together, making it easier for Natan to hit multiple targets with his skills and maximize his damage.
- Angela: Angela's ultimate skill, Heart of Angel, provides a powerful shield, making Natan even more difficult to take down.

counters.counter_desc: Gloo is a support hero with low mobility, making him an easy target for Natan's attacks. Natan can easily outmaneuver Gloo and deal significant damage.
- Yu Zhong: Yu Zhong is a fighter hero who is slow and lacks mobility, making him an easy target for Natan's attacks. Natan can easily kite Yu Zhong and deal high damage.
- Barats: Barats is a tank hero with low mobility, making him an easy target for Natan's kiting and damage. Natan can easily outmaneuver Barats and deal significant damage.
- Tigreal: Tigreal is a tank hero who relies on his ultimate skill to control enemies. Natan can use his mobility to avoid Tigreal's ultimate and continue dealing damage.
- Phoveus: Phoveus is a tank hero with limited mobility, making him vulnerable to Natan's attacks. Natan can easily kite Phoveus and deal high damage.

dominants.dominant_desc: This section should be tailored to the specific hero you are using.  Include advice on skill usage, item builds, and optimal strategies for laning and team fights in the early game.
dominants.dominant_title: This guide focuses on maximizing your impact in the early game of Mobile Legends, specifically before reaching level 4. This is a crucial period where smart decisions can significantly influence the o

emblems.emblem_desc: The Mage Emblem is the best choice for Natan because it boosts his magic damage and adaptive attack. It enhances his early game poke and scales well into the late game. Recommended talents include Agility for movement speed, Weapons Master for improved scaling, and Lethal Ignition or Magic Worship for extra burn damage during combos. This setup maximizes his burst potential and mobility, making him harder to catch and deadlier in fights.

equips.equip_desc: The build is chosen to balance high burst damage, skill spamming, and survivability, making Natan deadly but still able to last in fights.

hero.hero_overview: Natan is a high-mobility, magic damage marksman with a unique reverse mechanic through his ultimate. He excels at kiting and positioning in fights.
hero.hero_strength: Natan has strong laning potential in the Gold Lane due to his ranged poke and scaling damage. He excels in mid to late game thanks to his high attack speed and mobility, which boost both damage and survivability. His unique ultimate allows him to outplay enemies and reposition quickly in team fights. Natan is especially effective at kiting melee heroes and can melt through both squishy and tanky targets with his magic DPS build.
hero.hero_weakness: Natan has a weak early game and relies heavily on items to become effective. He is vulnerable to crowd control and burst damage, especially before building defensive items. His skills require good aim and timing, and his reverse clone ultimate can be confusing for new players. Poor positioning or mistimed ultimates can leave him exposed during fights, making him less forgiving than other marksmen.

data.recommend: This is my own opinion top cotabato natan

spell.spell_desc: Flicker is the best Battle Spell for Natan. It provides an instant blink that helps him escape dangerous situations, reposition in team fights, or chase down fleeing enemies. Since Natan is squishy and lacks innate escape tools, Flicker greatly increases his survivability. Use it to dodge crowd control, escape ambushes, or blink forward while using your ultimate to confuse and burst enemies.

dominants.dominant_desc: At the start of the game always go to the purple buff and hit the two thunder fenrir to immediately stack up your passive if done right you can have 5 stacks after killing the buff and the extra movement speed will allow you to quickly go to the red buff and once there go inside the molten fiend and try to hit the lava golem to stack up your passive

If there is no enemies around and you have the item swift ******** use your ultimate to kill the turtle position your ultimate behind the turtle so your attacks from your and the clone will be faster but if you think there is enemies around position the ultimate where it is safe to escape

Natan should always go in last in a team fight as he is a glass cannon and positioning is crucial especially in the late game where a death can cause the entire game always position your ultimate at a safe location to safely teleport out 
If the enemy team is in front of you and you are trying to chase position the ultimate in front of you as if you walk forward the clone will move backward dont always immediately use teleport when trying to chase or youll easily get caught
Natan with his clone can easily clear out the lord use you ultimate when the lord is near and teleport out to a safe position

emblems.emblem_desc: Mage emblem will add that 30 magic power which like 1 minor item at the early game and killing spree will ensure that much needed sustain to Natan as lifesteal items arent really suitable to his build and seasoned hunter will boost your jungle clear

equips.equip_desc: Arcane boots magic pen will boost your early jungle clear until you get swift crossbow and after feather of heaven starlium scythe will give you that true damage and lifesteal and blood wings will give you that shield while also increasing movement speed once its broken give you a chance to sustain and to reposition while the holy crystal will give you that massive magic power and divine glaive for peneration
Almost the same build from before but with concentrated energy instead of holy crystal it will increase your lifesteal and magic power but not as much to holy crystal

hero.hero_overview:
Role
Natan is a *Marksman/Attack Damage Carry (ADC)* hero, exceling at dealing physical damage from a distance.

Farming Route
A common farming route for Natan is:

1. *Top lane*: Start by farming the top lane, focusing on the minion waves and jungle creeps.
2. *Jungle*: Once you've farmed the top lane, head into the jungle to farm the monster camps.
3. *Bottom lane*: After farming the jungle, rotate to the bottom lane to farm the minion waves and harass the enemy tower.

Tips
1. *Positioning*: Natan excels at dealing damage from a distance, so try to stay at a safe distance from enemies.
2. *Use Shadowstep wisely*: Natan's Shadowstep ability allows him to quickly reposition himself. Use it to escape danger or chase down enemies.
3. *Focus on one target*: Natan's damage output is highest when focusing on a single target. Try to pick off one enemy at a time.
4. *Use ultimate ability in team fights*: Natan's ultimate ability, Shadow Realm, allows him to deal rapid attacks while remaining stealthy. Use it in team fights to deal massive damage.

Item Build
1. *Attack damage items*: Focus on building items that increase Natan's attack damage, such as Scarlet Phantom, Windtalker, and Malefic Roar.
2. *Critical strike chance*: Building critical strike chance items, such as Berseker's Fury and Endless Battle, can greatly increase Natan's damage output.
3. *Movement speed*: Building movement speed items, such as Swift Boots and Rapid Boots, can help Natan quickly reposition himself and chase down enemies.

Emblems
1. *Marksman emblem*: Use the Marksman emblem to increase Natan's attack damage and critical strike chance.
2. *Custom emblem*: You can also use a custom emblem with a mix of attack damage, critical strike chance, and movement speed.

Battle Spell
1. *Flicker*: Use Flicker to quickly reposition yourself.

hero.hero_overview:
Natan gains a stack of Entanglement each time he or his Reverse-Clone hits an enemy with a skill (up to 6 stacks). Each stack lasts 5s and grants 7.5% Attack Speed and 7.5% Movement Speed.

Natan's Basic Attack projectiles will return to Natan after reaching the maximum range, dealing an extra 30 (+50% Total Physical Attack) (+65% Total Magic Power) Magic Damage to enemies along the way (inherits a portion of Attack Effects
hero.hero_strength: Upgrade Skill 1 at Level 1 and prioritize upgrading Skill 1 with Skill 2 as backup. Upgrade the Ultimate whenever it is available.In teamfights, summon a Reverse-Clone with Natan's Ultimate, then use his 2nd Skill followed by his 1st Skill to knockback and damage enemies, and finish with a barrage of Basic Attacks. Depending on the situation, Natan's Ultimate can also be used to chase or escape
hero.hero_weakness: Natan is countered by heroes with targeted control and high burst like Saber and Eudora that can lock him down and finish him off.
